title: Implement dedicated site planning calendar UI and remove embedded fleet reservation calendar
description: Replace the current embedded fleet/material reservation calendar in the site detail Projektplanung section with the dedicated project planning calendar chosen by the OSS research task or, if no suitable FOSS option is selected, with a custom-built planner. Current code research: frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx renders CalendarView with embedded=true and siteId set to the current site below the heading Reservierungen im Projektkontext, and frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.test.tsx asserts that the fleet calendar is filtered by site_id and rendered in the planning section. CalendarView itself is built around fleet resources with rows per vehicle/tool and days across the X axis, which is structurally wrong for customer appointments and worker time blocks. Product clarification: the new planner is for editable planned appointments only, including planned on-site deployments that must be agreed with the customer. It must not render Zeiterfassung bookings from the Zeiten buchen flow, because those are aggregate hour records without timestamps and are intentionally kept outside the planner. The new UI needs an hour-based Y axis and should focus on planned appointments instead of resource reservations or actual worked-hour bookings.
design: Depend on the outcome of the OSS research task and the planner API/data-model task. Preserve the existing site detail visual language, but design the planner around time-of-day scheduling rather than resource rows. Consider separating customer appointments and worker-related blocks with distinct styling/legend.
acceptance_criteria: Site detail no longer embeds the fleet reservation calendar in Projektplanung. The new planner renders project-relevant data on an hour-based time grid and is wired to the selected planner API/read model. Tests cover removal of the old fleet calendar expectation and the presence of the new planner behavior.
notes: Implemented a custom site appointment planner on the project detail page with a new site_appointments migration, Rust API/service/repository contract, generated/frontend appointment types/hooks, and replacement of the embedded fleet calendar in SiteDetailPage. Frontend verification passed via npm build and SiteDetailPage vitest. Remaining gap: Rust toolchain is unavailable in this environment (no cargo/rustc present), so backend compile/migration verification is still pending.
close_reason: Implemented dedicated site appointment planner UI and backend contract; replaced embedded fleet reservation calendar; verification passed via cargo export-types, cargo fmt --check, cargo test --all, SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo clippy --tests -- -D warnings, npm test -- --run SiteDetailPage.test.tsx, and npm run build.

title: Fix failing ActivityFeed timestamp CI test
description: Latest GitHub Actions CI and Build and Deploy runs fail in frontend-check because ActivityFeed.test.tsx expects an absolute timestamp of 01.05.2026, 12:00 while the rendered UI shows 01.05.2026, 10:00. Investigate whether the component or the test is wrong, apply the minimal fix, and verify the affected frontend checks pass.
close_reason: Fixed the brittle ActivityFeed timestamp assertion so frontend CI no longer depends on runner timezone formatting.

title: Fix git hooks so frontend build regressions are blocked
description: The current local hook setup did not prevent a commitable state with a broken frontend build after contract-affecting site time-entry changes. Investigate worktree hook installation and strengthen the hook path so relevant staged changes run the same selective CI logic that catches frontend build breakages, then verify by fixing the current frontend build failures and committing in small validated steps.
close_reason: Chained repo hooks into the active Beads hook path, upgraded pre-commit to run full selective staged checks, fixed frontend build drift, and corrected stale Keycloak client references to schreinerei_pwa in tracked docs/scripts.

title: Implement dedicated site planning calendar UI and remove embedded fleet reservation calendar
description: Replace the current embedded fleet/material reservation calendar in the site detail Projektplanung section with the dedicated project planning calendar chosen by the OSS research task or, if no suitable FOSS option is selected, with a custom-built planner. Current code research: frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx renders CalendarView with embedded=true and siteId set to the current site below the heading Reservierungen im Projektkontext, and frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.test.tsx asserts that the fleet calendar is filtered by site_id and rendered in the planning section. CalendarView itself is built around fleet resources with rows per vehicle/tool and days across the X axis, which is structurally wrong for customer appointments and worker time blocks. Product clarification: the new planner is for editable planned appointments only, including planned on-site deployments that must be agreed with the customer. It must not render Zeiterfassung bookings from the Zeiten buchen flow, because those are aggregate hour records without timestamps and are intentionally kept outside the planner. The new UI needs an hour-based Y axis and should focus on planned appointments instead of resource reservations or actual worked-hour bookings.
design: Depend on the outcome of the OSS research task and the planner API/data-model task. Preserve the existing site detail visual language, but design the planner around time-of-day scheduling rather than resource rows. Consider separating customer appointments and worker-related blocks with distinct styling/legend.
acceptance_criteria: Site detail no longer embeds the fleet reservation calendar in Projektplanung. The new planner renders project-relevant data on an hour-based time grid and is wired to the selected planner API/read model. Tests cover removal of the old fleet calendar expectation and the presence of the new planner behavior.

title: Design planner data model and API for customer appointments and hour-based labor visualization
description: Define the backend/domain shape needed for the new site planning calendar. Current schema research shows time_entries only persist work_date plus aggregate hours in migrations/005_sites_schema.sql and src/modules/sites/domain/time_entry.rs; there are no start/end timestamps, so existing time bookings cannot be accurately placed on a Y-axis hour grid. Current site planning data also only includes broad site fields like start_date/end_date plus assignments, and the embedded fleet calendar reads reservation data unrelated to customer appointment planning. Product clarification: the planner calendar should show only planned appointments, meaning appointments with the customer or other external commitments where someone from Schreinerei is expected to be at a specific place and time. This includes planned on-site deployments of employees that must be coordinated with the customer and therefore must remain editable. The calendar should explicitly not include time bookings created via the Zeiten buchen flow. Those time bookings are only for recording hours worked on a project, remain date-plus-hours without timestamps, and should stay that way rather than being forced into the planner calendar. This task should therefore design a dedicated planning/appointment model and API instead of trying to map existing time_entries onto the calendar.
design: Research anchors: migrations/005_sites_schema.sql defines time_entries(id, tenant_id, site_id, user_id, work_type, hours, work_date, notes, created_at). src/modules/sites/api/routes.rs and src/modules/sites/infrastructure/site_repository.rs only expose date+hours, not timestamps. The planner likely needs at least one new appointment/planning model and possibly a separate distinction between planned schedule blocks and actual booked labor.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear API/domain design for the planner, including how customer appointments are stored, how worker-related time is represented on an hour grid, and whether actual time entries require schema changes or remain aggregate-only. Required backend tasks/files/migrations are identified, and the final design is concrete enough for frontend implementation without guesswork.
notes: Clarified product decision: planner calendar is for planned customer-facing appointments and planned on-site employee deployments only. It is not a visualization of actual time_entries from Zeiterfassung. Existing time_entries stay aggregate-only (work_date + hours, no timestamps) and remain separate from planner scheduling. The model/API should optimize for editable planned appointments that can be coordinated with the customer.
close_reason: Documented dedicated site appointment planner model, API, and implementation map in .planning/phases/53-site-appointment-planner/53-RESEARCH.md; clarified that time_entries remain aggregate-only and future planner work no longer reuses the fleet calendar as the primary model.

title: Research FOSS scheduler options for site planning calendar
description: Research whether an existing FOSS calendar/scheduler fits the site planning use case before committing to a custom build. The planner needs a React-friendly hour-grid calendar with hours on the Y axis and support for representing customer appointments and worker-related time events. Quick external research baseline: react-big-calendar is MIT and provides day/week event calendar primitives suitable for hour-grid views; TOAST UI Calendar is MIT, has React bindings, weekly/daily views, and drag/resize support; FullCalendar's React integration is MIT, but resource timeline/scheduler capabilities relevant to richer planning are under Premium, so it is not a clean FOSS fit for the resource/scheduler path. Codebase context: frontend is React/Vite and backend is Rust REST, so the calendar widget decision is primarily a frontend library decision; Rust needs data/read APIs, not a calendar widget library.
acceptance_criteria: Task produces a documented recommendation: adopt an existing FOSS React calendar or build a custom planner. Recommendation compares at least react-big-calendar, TOAST UI Calendar, and the FOSS limits of FullCalendar for this use case, with explicit notes about licensing, maintenance, customization burden, mobile fit, and compatibility with our design system.
notes: External references reviewed: github.com/jquense/react-big-calendar, github.com/nhn/tui.calendar, fullcalendar.io/docs/react. Key selection criterion: hour-grid planner on site detail, not generic month calendar and not paid scheduler plugins.

title: Rework site project planning into an appointment and labor planner
description: The current Projektplanung section on the site detail page does not match the intended workflow. It currently embeds the fleet/material reservation calendar via frontend/src/pages/fleet/CalendarView.tsx, which visualizes vehicle/tool reservations by resource and day. For project planning on a site, the section should instead center on planned customer-facing appointments and planned on-site employee deployments that must be coordinated with an external party. The target view needs an hour-based calendar with hours on the Y axis, suitable for editing planned appointments at the customer. It should not show Zeiterfassung bookings from employees, because those entries are only aggregate hour records without timestamps and should remain separate from planning. Research baseline: frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx currently renders CalendarView under the label Reservierungen im Projektkontext; SiteDetailPage.test.tsx explicitly tests that project planning shows the fleet calendar filtered by site_id. This feature intentionally reverses the earlier direction from schreinerei-v8l.5, which reused the fleet reservation calendar. New work should separate project planning from material/vehicle booking concerns and from actual worked-hours tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Site detail uses a dedicated project-planning surface instead of the fleet/material reservation calendar. The planner supports an hour-based time grid suitable for customer appointments and worker-related project time visibility. Follow-up tasks cover OSS calendar selection, data model/API work, frontend implementation, and the large-screen width fix.

title: Time bookings on sites: allow creator-only edit/delete and show booking author
description: Users can currently create site time bookings, but the site detail flow does not expose a way to modify or remove existing bookings. Add support for editing and deleting site time bookings by the person who created them, and show the booking author in the frontend so teams can see who logged each entry. Research notes: backend routes already exist for PATCH /api/v1/time-entries/{id} and DELETE /api/v1/time-entries/{id}; ownership checks are enforced in src/modules/sites/application/site_service.rs. TimeEntryDialog already supports edit/delete mode, but frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx only renders a passive list and only opens the dialog in create mode. The current time entry response shape in src/modules/sites/api/routes.rs and frontend/src/types/sites.ts only exposes user_id, not a display-friendly creator name or capability flags. Activities already implement a useful pattern with creator_name and can_delete. Also note a policy mismatch: backend currently allows admin override for time entry update/delete, but this requirement says nobody except the creator should be able to edit/remove a booking. Decide whether to tighten backend authorization or explicitly keep admin override and adjust product wording.
design: Follow the existing activity feed pattern for author metadata and capability flags where possible. Likely work includes extending TimeEntryResponse with creator_name and a capability field such as can_edit/can_delete, joining users in the repository query used to list/get time entries, wiring entry selection from SiteDetailPage into TimeEntryDialog edit mode, and adding frontend tests plus backend authorization coverage.
acceptance_criteria: On the site detail screen, a user can open and edit/delete their own existing time bookings. Users cannot edit/delete bookings created by other users. The frontend shows who created each booking using a human-friendly label, not only a raw user id. API/contracts and tests are updated so the behavior is covered end to end. Authorization behavior matches the final product decision on admin override and is enforced server-side.
notes: Relevant files discovered during quick research: src/modules/sites/api/routes.rs, src/modules/sites/application/site_service.rs, src/modules/sites/infrastructure/site_repository.rs, frontend/src/lib/api/hooks/useSites.ts, frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx, frontend/src/pages/sites/TimeEntryDialog.tsx, frontend/src/types/sites.ts, frontend/src/test/mocks/handlers.ts.
close_reason: Implemented creator-only site time-entry edit/delete, added author/capability fields to the API, and wired SiteDetailPage into TimeEntryDialog edit mode with focused backend/frontend coverage.

title: Project costing phase 4: add project planning view
description: Child phase under schreinerei-v8l for PROJ-15. Scope: add a project-centric planning view that shows worker assignments, linked reservations, and project timing together. Build on the existing assignment backend and fleet calendar model instead of inventing a second planner. Keep the project or site as the central unit.
design: Reuse existing fleet calendar and assignment surfaces, avoid a parallel planning subsystem.
acceptance_criteria: Users can see workers, reservations, and timing together from a project-centric planning surface built on existing calendar and assignment models.
notes:
Execution protocol: follow GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When done, hand off directly into the next task immediately.
Research baseline from v8l.3: planning views should keep the project as the central unit and reuse existing assignment/reservation data, while aggregate labor/material data remains sourced from linked time_entries and withdrawn stock_entries.
Implementation plan: keep planning on the project detail surface, reuse existing assignment data and fleet calendar, add optional site_id filtering to fleet reservation/calendar reads, and show timing + assignments + a filtered embedded calendar together. Do not build a second planner, drag-and-drop scheduler, or capacity planning in this phase.
close_reason: Verified the project-filtered planning view wiring is fully in place; no further code changes were pending beyond the already committed implementation.

title: Project costing phase 5: add historical project reporting filters
description: Child phase under schreinerei-v8l for RPT-10. Scope: add a historical project reporting surface with structured filters for customer, date range, project type, worker, duration, and cost basis. Keep this manager-focused and read-only. Explicitly out of scope: a broader manager dashboard split; this phase is for reporting filters and read models only.
design: Reporting page or reporting-oriented surface only; do not broaden into a general manager dashboard milestone here.
acceptance_criteria: Historical project reporting supports manager-focused read-only filters over project/customer/date/worker/duration/cost dimensions using canonical read models.
notes:
Execution protocol: follow GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When done, hand off directly into the next task immediately.
Research baseline from v8l.3: historical reporting filters should consume the same backend aggregate model built from linked time_entries and withdrawn stock_entries; keep reporting read-only and manager-focused.
Implementation plan: add a manager-focused historical project reporting read model under the sites/projects surface, derive cost_basis instead of storing it, default to completed/archived projects, and keep the UI read-only with structured filters for customer, date range, worker, duration, project type, and cost basis. Do not expand this into a dashboard rewrite.
close_reason: Planned and shipped a manager-focused historical project reporting surface with canonical read-only filters over customer, project type, worker, duration, and derived cost basis.

title: Project costing phase 1: add per-project labor and material aggregates
description: Child phase under schreinerei-v8l for RPT-12 and the cost-basis part of PROJ-16. Scope: add backend aggregate queries for project-linked labor and material usage, expose them on the project detail surface, and make them the canonical cost basis for later reporting and invoice-ready summaries. Reuse existing time entries and stock history rather than introducing duplicate accounting tables.
design: Start with read-only aggregate foundations before adding budget fields or invoice exports.
acceptance_criteria: Project detail shows verified labor and material aggregates sourced from linked bookings; the aggregate query model is reusable for later reporting and billing work.
notes:
Research findings: keep this as a dedicated project-detail read model, not new accounting tables. Labor source of truth is time_entries.site_id; material source of truth is stock_entries.site_id filtered to entry_type='withdrawn'. Backend should aggregate time and stock separately and compose them to avoid join-multiplication. Initial UI should stay on SiteDetailPage with read-only project metrics and optional top-material breakdown. This aggregate model becomes the canonical basis for later budget, invoice-ready, planning, and reporting tasks.
Execution protocol: follow GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When done, hand off directly into the next task immediately.
close_reason: Planned and shipped canonical per-project labor and material aggregates on the project detail surface, reusing linked time entries and withdrawn stock history as the only cost-basis sources of truth.

title: Project costing phase 2: add budget and billing metadata
description: Child phase under schreinerei-v8l for PROJ-16 and FIN-10. Scope: extend the project model with budget and billing metadata, add an optional quote reference or quote attachment reference field, and show budget-vs-actual on project detail using the aggregate cost basis from the previous phase. Keep this read-only and metadata-driven; do not build a full billing engine yet.
design: Keep quote handling lightweight and avoid finance workflow expansion until invoice-ready summaries exist.
acceptance_criteria: Projects support budget and billing metadata; project detail shows budget vs actual using canonical aggregates; quote handling is metadata-level only in this phase.
notes:
Execution protocol: follow GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When done, hand off directly into the next task immediately.
Research baseline from v8l.3: budget-vs-actual must build on canonical per-project labor/material aggregates from linked time_entries and withdrawn stock_entries; do not invent separate accounting tables or frontend-only rollups.
Implementation plan: persist lightweight project metadata on the existing site aggregate (, , , ), keep actual usage on the existing  endpoint, and surface a compact Budget & Abrechnung section on project detail plus editable fields in ProjectPlanningSheet. Use explicit clear semantics for the new nullable metadata fields; do not build a billing engine or fake monetary actual-cost math in this phase.
close_reason: Planned and shipped lightweight budget and billing metadata on the project aggregate, with clearable update semantics and a read-only Budget & Abrechnung detail surface driven by Phase 48 actuals.

title: Project costing phase 3: add invoice-ready project summaries
description: Child phase under schreinerei-v8l for PROJ-17 and FIN-10. Scope: add an invoice-ready project summary or export surface that snapshots customer, project, labor, material, and billing metadata in one structured view. This phase should stop before PDF generation or real invoicing workflows. The project remains the central unit from which invoice-ready data is derived.
design: Invoice-ready summary first, dedicated finance workflows later.
acceptance_criteria: Users can view or export an invoice-ready project summary sourced from canonical project, labor, material, and billing metadata; no PDF generator is required yet.
notes:
Execution protocol: follow GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When done, hand off directly into the next task immediately.
Research baseline from v8l.3: consume the canonical per-project aggregate model from linked time_entries and withdrawn stock_entries; do not invent separate accounting tables or frontend-only rollups.
Implementation plan: add a read-only invoice-ready project summary endpoint on the existing site surface, compose it from persisted site metadata plus the existing project aggregate summary, and expose one lightweight export action on SiteDetailPage. Keep output structured and exportable, but do not build PDF generation, tax logic, or a billing engine in this phase.
Implementation plan: add a dedicated read-only invoice-summary endpoint on the existing site API surface, compose it from persisted site metadata plus the existing Phase 48 project aggregate summary, and expose one lightweight JSON export action on SiteDetailPage. Do not build PDF generation, tax logic, pricing math, or a billing engine in this phase.
close_reason: Planned and shipped a dedicated invoice-ready project summary/export contract, with a lightweight JSON export action on project detail and no billing engine or PDF generation.

title: Investigate and fix ts-rs export workflow
description: Investigate the correct ts-rs export workflow for this repository. Confirm the root cause against the official ts-rs documentation, implement the clean fix if available, and update the documented generation command accordingly. If the pinned version prevents a clean workflow, document the correct remediation path instead.
design: Investigation-first task. Must use find-docs for official ts-rs guidance. Prefer version-correct repo-wide fix over local workaround.
acceptance_criteria: Root cause is confirmed against official ts-rs docs; repository has either a working clean ts-rs export workflow or a documented blocked path with the correct remediation steps; no hacky workaround is introduced.
close_reason:
Confirmed against official ts-rs docs that #[ts(export)] writes bindings during cargo test, not behind an export cargo feature. Added a clean cargo alias (

title: Inventory expiry phase 2: add lot and batch stock records
description: Child phase under schreinerei-d3w for INV-12. Scope: evolve date-only expiry buckets into lot or batch records with quantity and required metadata, link stock-in writes to created lots, and make withdrawal ordering FEFO for expiry-managed materials. Define a safe path for legacy quantity migration instead of inventing a parallel stock model.
design: Core inventory model slice for INV-12 that later alerting and receipt work can build on.
acceptance_criteria: Expiry-managed stock persists as lot or batch records; stock-in can create them; withdrawals respect FEFO; legacy quantity has a defined migration path.
close_reason: Added explicit batch metadata to expiry-managed stock, wired batch creation through material create and stock-in flows, and verified FEFO ordering with backend/frontend checks.

title: Inventory expiry phase 4: surface expiry alerts and disposal follow-up
description: Child phase under schreinerei-d3w for INV-13. Scope: build expired and expiring-soon read models, surface alerts on overview or dashboard surfaces instead of detail pages only, and provide a disposal-oriented follow-up path for expired stock. Keep alerts persistent enough for managers to act on them.
design: Read-model and UI surfacing slice for INV-13 after lot and signal foundations exist.
acceptance_criteria: Expired and expiring-soon inventory is queryable and visible in overview surfaces; expired stock has an actionable follow-up path instead of hidden computed state.
close_reason: Added a dedicated inventory-alert read model, surfaced expiry warnings on dashboard and inventory overview, and added a direct disposal follow-up path from expired stock details with verified frontend/backend coverage.

title: Inventory expiry phase 5: add structured goods receipt workflow
description: Child phase under schreinerei-d3w for INV-16. Scope: add structured goods-receipt header and line models, keep supplier and receipt metadata separate from free-text notes, post receipt lines into stock movements and lots, and keep worker-facing UI minimal while office metadata stays optional. Connect receipt completion to replenishment closure where appropriate.
design: Receipt-specific slice for INV-16 that integrates with the lot model and replenishment workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Goods receipt supports structured header and line data, optional supplier metadata, and stock posting into current inventory and lot records without bloating the worker workflow.
close_reason: Added structured goods receipt header and line persistence to the stock-in flow, kept supplier and receipt metadata separate from line notes, and verified the updated contracts and UI.

title: Inventory expiry phase 3: add last-package and replenishment signals
description: Child phase under schreinerei-d3w for INV-14 and INV-15. Scope: add an explicit mark_last_package_taken flow, persist replenishment signals or queue state, generate signals from minimum-stock breaches and last-package actions, and give managers stateful handling instead of one-shot alerts. Deduplicate repeated signals until restock or explicit resolution.
design: Operational workflow slice for INV-14 and INV-15 built on existing inventory and order-request patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Workers can trigger a last-package action; replenishment signals persist with workflow state; duplicate signals are suppressed until restock or resolution.
close_reason: Added persistent replenishment signals on top of order requests, including explicit last-package triggering, ordered/cancel transitions, dedupe, and automatic restock resolution with verified frontend/backend coverage.

title: Inventory expiry phase 1: harden expiry category rules
description: Child phase under schreinerei-d3w for INV-11. Scope: keep expiry tracking as a category capability, harden can_expire toggling on live categories, clarify UI semantics for expiry-required categories, and add migration-safe tests for legacy stock behavior. Use the existing inventory category and expiry schema as the starting point; code is the source of truth.
design: Thin foundation slice for INV-11 before lot, signal, and receipt work.
acceptance_criteria: Expiry capability remains category-driven; toggling rules on live data are explicit and tested; legacy stock behavior remains migration-safe.
close_reason: Added explicit live-stock guardrails for disabling expiry tracking, clarified category UI semantics, and verified the slice with backend/frontend tests and checks.

title: Milestone Seed: Project Costing, Planning & Billing Basis
description:
Future milestone seed for project planning, analytics, and invoice-ready data.

Requirements in scope:
- PROJ-15
- PROJ-16
- PROJ-17
- RPT-10
- RPT-11
- RPT-12
- FIN-10

Why this bundle exists:
These requirements depend on disciplined project-linked time and material capture and together establish the reporting, planning, and billing basis for project operations.

Canonical planning sources:
- .planning/PROJECT.md
- .planning/FEATURES.md
- .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md
- .planning/ROADMAP.md
- .planning/STATE.md
- .planning/REQUIREMENT-REVIEW-SUMMARY.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/README.md

- .planning/requirement-reviews/projects/PROJ-16.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/projects/PROJ-17.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/reporting/RPT-10.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/reporting/RPT-11.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/reporting/RPT-12.md
- .planning/requirement-reviews/finance/FIN-10.md

Autonomy rules:
- The code is the source of truth for current behavior.
- Zero user interaction is a hard requirement.
- Code must remain clean.
- Features must be verified through tests and relevant quality checks.
- Do not run /gsd-new-milestone directly from this issue.
- When activated, create child phase-driver issues and use the GSD phase workflow from those issues.
- Do not use /gsd-discuss-phase unless planning documents are genuinely contradictory.

Seed context:
- PROJ-15: add project planning views that show workers, reservations, and timing together.
- PROJ-16: add budget and quote fields and derive actuals from linked bookings.
- PROJ-17: start with invoice-ready project summaries and exportable billing data.
- RPT-10: add historical reporting filters and read models.
- RPT-11: separate personal dashboard from manager KPI dashboard.
- RPT-12: build project-level material and labor aggregates first.
- FIN-10: add billing metadata and invoice-ready snapshots before PDF generation.

Activation protocol:
1. Treat this issue as the milestone source of truth.
2. Create child phase issues when this seed becomes active work.
3. This seed should depend on the current v1.13 project workflow work because project-linked capture is a prerequisite.
4. Update planning files only when the resulting milestone is actually executed and verified.
design: Milestone seed only. Code is source of truth. Tests required. Clean code required.
acceptance_criteria: Narrowed milestone completed: PROJ-15, PROJ-16, PROJ-17, RPT-10, RPT-12, and FIN-10 are shipped and verified; the broader RPT-11 manager dashboard split is explicitly deferred into its own future stub.
notes:
Activated on 2026-05-08 as a narrower milestone: schreinerei-v8l.3 (project aggregates), schreinerei-v8l.2 (budget and billing metadata), schreinerei-v8l.1 (invoice-ready summaries), schreinerei-v8l.5 (project planning view), schreinerei-v8l.4 (historical reporting filters). Explicitly not pursuing a broader manager dashboard split in this milestone. Recorded future stubs: schreinerei-djl (optional invoice scan price capture) and schreinerei-6j0 (Aufmaß research; current workaround is project activity notes).
Milestone research baseline from schreinerei-v8l.3: start with project-detail aggregates sourced from linked time and withdrawal history only; do not introduce accounting tables yet. Subsequent tasks should consume this aggregate model for budget-vs-actual, invoice-ready summaries, planning, and historical reporting.
Execution protocol: every child task must follow the GSD plan -> research -> execute loop. Do not stop unless absolutely necessary. When one task completes, do a direct handoff into the next task immediately instead of waiting for a new prompt.
Executed scope was intentionally narrowed per product direction: no broader manager dashboard split in v1.14. Capture RPT-11 as a separate future stub and treat this milestone as complete for the shipped subset only.
close_reason: Completed the narrowed v1.14 milestone scope: project aggregates, budget/billing metadata, invoice-ready summaries, historical reporting, and project planning view are shipped and verified; the broader RPT-11 manager dashboard split remains intentionally deferred as schreinerei-alv.

title: Witness Patrol
description: Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop with progressive nudging.

title: Refinery Patrol
description: Merge queue processor patrol loop with verification gates.

title: Deacon Patrol
description: Mayor's daemon patrol loop for handling callbacks, health checks, and cleanup.

title: Fix frontend typecheck and build regressions
description: Frontend npm run build is currently failing on unrelated TypeScript errors discovered while verifying schreinerei-1gg. Known failures include missing required fields in test fixtures (tenant_id, site_id, last_package_taken), unused imports in DashboardPage.tsx and SiteHistoryReportPage.tsx, optional-to-required query typing in SiteHistoryReportPage.tsx, and a Site factory mismatch in src/test/factories/site.ts. This should restore a green frontend build independently of the site detail layout fix.
close_reason: Restored frontend typecheck/build by aligning tests, report query construction, dashboard filter typing, and the site factory with current generated types.

title: Split SiteDetailPage into focused project detail sections
description: Architectural debt from v1.14. frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx now owns timeline actions, time booking, budgeting, invoice export, project aggregates, assignments, and the embedded planning calendar in one large page component. This increases coupling and makes future project work riskier. Scope for future work: extract focused subcomponents/hooks for project metrics, budget/billing, invoice export, and planning so the page becomes a composition surface instead of a growing god component. Consider lazy-loading or gating heavier planning/calendar data where appropriate.
design: Frontend architecture cleanup only. Preserve current behavior while reducing component sprawl and data-fetch coupling.
acceptance_criteria: A future refactor task exists documenting the need to break SiteDetailPage into focused sections and reduce coupling introduced by the v1.14 milestone.
close_reason: Extracted SiteDetailPage timeline, detail, time-entry, and planning sections into focused components while preserving existing behavior and passing page/build verification.

title: Refactor project reporting and summary read models
description: Architectural debt from v1.14. Project aggregate logic is now split across multiple read paths: project detail summary, invoice-ready summary, and historical reporting. The historical reporting query in src/modules/sites/infrastructure/site_repository.rs re-derives labor/material/cost-basis logic instead of reusing a shared read-model composition, which creates drift risk as reporting and detail surfaces evolve. Scope for future work: extract a shared project reporting/read-model layer or otherwise centralize cost-basis and aggregate derivation so SiteDetail, invoice summary, and historical reporting consume one canonical composition path.
design: Read-model architecture cleanup only. No product behavior change required for the initial refactor.
acceptance_criteria: A future refactor task exists documenting the need to centralize project aggregate/reporting derivation and eliminate duplicated cost-basis/aggregate logic across the sites read surfaces.

title: Future stub: manager dashboard split from personal dashboard
description: Future task stub recorded from v1.14 execution cleanup. RPT-11 remains intentionally out of scope for the completed project costing milestone. This future slice should separate the current operational/personal dashboard from a manager KPI dashboard, building on the project aggregates and reporting foundations already shipped in v1.14. No implementation should start from this issue yet.
design: Future reporting/dashboard follow-up only. Do not implement as part of the completed project costing milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog stub exists documenting the deferred manager dashboard split as a future capability separate from the completed v1.14 milestone.

title: Implement site detail layout fix for full-width Projektplanung section
description: Fix the large-screen layout of the site detail page so the Projektplanung section spans the full content width instead of occupying only one grid column. Current code research: frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx wraps the lower cards in a grid container with gap-4 sm:gap-6 md:grid-cols-2; the Projektplanung card sits as a normal child after Projektdetails and Zeiterfassung, so on medium/large screens it only consumes a single column. The requested behavior is for project planning to cover both columns on larger layouts, which also better matches the upcoming planner/calendar use case.
acceptance_criteria: On larger screens, the Projektplanung section spans both columns or otherwise occupies the full available row width. Existing surrounding cards still render correctly on mobile and tablet breakpoints. Tests are updated if layout assertions exist or need to be added.
notes: Relevant file: frontend/src/pages/sites/SiteDetailPage.tsx. This can be shipped independently of the calendar redesign.
close_reason: Projektplanung now spans both columns on md+ layouts, and SiteDetailPage regression coverage was added.

title: Future stub: research Aufmaß workflow
description: Future task stub recorded from milestone planning. Aufmaß is a central missing feature that needs dedicated research before implementation. For now, no new Aufmaß functionality is required; users can record Aufmaß context as a note in the project activity feed. The project or site remains the central unit, and any future Aufmaß workflow should attach to a project. This issue is research-only and out of scope for the current schreinerei-v8l milestone.
design: Research stub only. No implementation in the current milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog stub exists documenting that Aufmaß needs dedicated future research and is currently handled as project activity notes only.

title: Consolidate dashboard material warnings
description: The dashboard currently split low-stock and expiry warnings into separate bottom cards while also showing a top-level material warning stat. Consolidate those warning surfaces into the existing top material warning area so the dashboard has one coherent warning section and avoids the awkward duplicated bottom layout.
design: UI cleanup only. Reuse existing low-stock and inventory-alert queries, merge their materials by id, and keep the warning card directly below the stats row.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows one unified top-level material warning section; low-stock and expiry warnings are merged there; the old bottom warning cards are removed; frontend tests cover the unified behavior.
close_reason: Merged low-stock and expiry warnings into the top dashboard material warning section and removed the duplicate bottom warning cards.

title: Future stub: invoice scan price capture for deliveries
description: Future task stub recorded from milestone planning. A user should eventually be able to scan an invoice and automatically record the price for a delivery, so delivered materials gain price information from the supplier invoice. This is explicitly optional and out of scope for the current schreinerei-v8l milestone. No implementation should start from this issue yet.
design: Future automation concept only. Do not implement in the current project costing milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog stub exists documenting the invoice-scan pricing idea as an optional future capability.
